steps.title: Pick easy values for the ratio
steps.content: Since $b$ is $89\%$ of $c$, choose $c=100$ and $b=89$. This keeps the ratio exact and avoids decimal algebra.

steps.title: Find a as a multiple of b
Calculate a/b and convert to percentage
steps.content:
Divide by $b=89$:
$$\frac{a}{b}=\frac{23\cdot89\cdot189}{100\cdot89}=\frac{23\cdot189}{100}=43.47$$
So $a$ is $43.47\cdot100\%=4{,}347\%$ of $b$. That matches choice D.
